task.title: Phase 1 Exercise: Environment Setup and Verification
task.shortdesc: Install DITA-OT, verify your environment, and run your first build to confirm everything works. This exercise is required for both technical writers and software engineers.

ul.li: An internet connection to download DITA-OT
A text/XML editor (Oxygen XML Editor recommended, or VS Code with XML extension)

context.p: This exercise takes you from zero to a working DITA environment. By the end, you will have DITA-OT installed, understand the basic command structure, and have produced your first HTML5 output from a DITA file.
Read first: Review the topics in Part 1 (Why Intelligent Content) and Part 2 (Prepare) of the training guide before starting.

step.cmd: Verify that Java 17 or later is installed.

info.p: DITA-OT 4.x requires Java 17+. Check your version:
If Java is not installed, download it from Adoptium (recommended) or your OS package manager.

stepresult.p: You should see output like: openjdk version "17.0.x" or higher.

step.cmd: Download DITA-OT 4.x from the official site.

info.p: Go to dita-ot.org/download and download the latest 4.x release ZIP.

substep.cmd: Extract the ZIP to a location you will remember (e.g., ~/dita-ot).
Add the bin/ directory to your system PATH.

info.codeblock: export PATH=$PATH:~/dita-ot/bin # Add this line to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc to make it permanent

step.cmd: Create a minimal DITA concept topic to test your setup.

info.p: Create a file named hello-dita.dita with this content:
info.codeblock:
Hello, DITA! My first DITA topic.

If you can read this in a browser, your DITA-OT setup works!

step.cmd: Build HTML5 output from your test topic.

stepresult.p: DITA-OT processes the file and creates out/hello/hello-dita.html.

step.cmd: Open the generated HTML file in your browser.

info.codeblock:
# Linux xdg-open out/hello/hello-dita.html
# macOS open out/hello/hello-dita.html
# Windows start out\hello\hello-dita.html

stepresult.p: You should see a rendered HTML page with the title "Hello, DITA!" and your paragraph text.

step.cmd: Explore available DITA-OT commands and transforms.

info.codeblock:
# List all available commands dita --help
# List installed transformation types dita transtypes
# List installed plugins dita plugins

stepresult.p: Note the available transforms (html5, pdf, etc.) and installed plugins. You will use these throughout the training.

p.b: Self-Check — you should now be able to:

ul.li: ✓ Create a basic DITA concept topic from scratch
✓ Build HTML5 output with a single command
✓ View the rendered output in a browser

result.note: Keep your hello-dita.dita file — you will expand it in Phase 2 exercises.
